pattern: The adversary orders losing transactions last, so you sum every net loss and then leave room for the single most expensive transaction to still be affordable when scheduled at the worst moment.
hints: A transaction only drains money permanently when its cost exceeds its cashback; the rest are net neutral or gains.
Imagine all the draining transactions done first, then ask which remaining transaction is hardest to afford right when your balance is lowest.

Let loss be the sum of max(0, cost minus cashback). For each transaction the requirement is that running loss, minus its own loss if it is a loser, plus its cost; take the maximum over all transactions.

edge_inputs.description: mix of losing and gaining transactions
all gainers so only the largest cost matters

stress.description: random transactions mixing losers and gainers
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    txns = []
    for _ in range(n):
        cost = rng.randint(0, 10**9)
        cash = rng.randint(0, 10**9)
        txns.append([cost, cash])
    return [txns]
stress.note: free mix of cost vs cashback exercises both loss branches
